title: Tide-Pool Truce: Extragradient Schedule Design for a Predator-Prey Saddle
strategies: trivial: zero step sizes -- take no steps, so the equilibrium-gradient norm stays at the starting value and the normalized score sits at the ~0.1 baseline floor
greedy: constant-step extragradient eta_k=gamma_k=1/||M||_2, the textbook stable choice; it converges but wastes the fixed budget by never accelerating or polishing

strong: simulate the exact update template internally and search the per-step (eta_k,gamma_k) schedule -- a constant-scale grid around 1/L followed by coordinate descent -- spending early steps aggressively and late steps as fine correction
invalid: emit non-finite (NaN/Inf) step sizes; the evaluator rejects the schedule and scores 0
